mnemonics.title: PUSH Sequence
mnemonics.content: CIA: Check Overflow → Increment Top → Add Element. Remember order for push operation

mnemonics.title: POP Priority
mnemonics.content: GRD: Get Value → Reset Space → Decrement Top. Sequence for safe popping

mnemonics.title: Top Initialization
mnemonics.content: -1 = Empty Hands: Top starts at -1 like empty hands waiting to hold plates

mnemonics.title: MAX Rule
mnemonics.content: Top's MAX-1 = Full. When top == MAX-1, stack is full (arrays start at 0)

mnemonics.title: LIFO Layers
mnemonics.content: Last In, First Out = Like layers in a cake – last layer added is first to be eaten

acronyms.usage: Core stack principle for insertion/removal order
Role of the top index in array management
Remember peek() checks top without removal

visualTips.visualization: Imagine stack as elevator – top index shows current floor. MAX is roof limit
Picture stack elements bursting through array's upper boundary like volcano eruption
Visualize trying to pull a plate from empty air (top = -1)
See top index as elevator button – push=↑ floors, pop=↓ floors
Like using a mirror to look at top plate without removing it

analogies.analogy: PEZ dispenser – candies (elements) are loaded/pushed and dispensed/popped from top
Limited shelf space – can't add books (elements) beyond shelf capacity
Library ladder position – determines which book (element) is accessible
Overfilled suitcase – zipper (MAX limit) won't close if you push more
Empty cookie jar – trying to pop when nothing's left
